base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN (33 passed / 0 failed across the qualification files at base+6-defects; 26 in the graded subset on the task image)
env_note: No task-specific env required. The CRE qualification suite is deterministic and offline; the JWT_SECRET_KEY baked into loangenus-repo:v1 is sufficient. No pydantic Settings ValidationError appears.

defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Visible tests use appraisals of 800k / 1.7M / 1.85M / 2.0M / 3.8M — all strictly > 100k, so >= and > agree; none lands on exactly 100k.

defects.edge_input: a primary FICO of exactly 850 (the band maximum), e.g. {"primary_fico_score": 850}
defects.wrong_output: drops the score (falls through to bureau / None) instead of returning 850
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Visible tests feed primary FICOs of 665 / 712 / 720 — never the 850 upper edge; only the exact-850 case differs.

defects.edge_input: an even number of bureau scores, e.g. {"bureau_fico_scores": {"experian": 660, "equifax": 700}}
defects.wrong_output: returns the lower-middle score (660) instead of the framework's upper-middle (700); identical on odd counts
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The only visible test that routes bureau scores through this resolver uses a single (odd) score; len//2 and (len-1)//2 coincide for all odd lengths.

defects.boundary_changed: v / 100.0 if v > 1 else v  ->  if v >= 1
defects.edge_input: tenant_concentration of exactly 1.0 (100% as a fraction), e.g. {"tenant_concentration": 1.0}
defects.wrong_output: returns 0.01 (treats 1.0 as the percent 1%) instead of 1.0
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No visible test supplies tenant_concentration or largest_tenant_pct at all.

defects.boundary_changed: if spent > explicit ...  ->  if spent >= explicit ...  (the CTC-rejection guard)
defects.edge_input: a stated total budget whose spend exactly equals it, with remaining present, e.g. {"construction_budget": 200_000, "spent_to_date": 200_000, "remaining_budget": 50_000}
defects.wrong_output: returns spent + remaining (250_000) instead of the stated total (200_000); only spend STRICTLY above the total should override it as a mislabeled cost-to-complete figure
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No visible test feeds an explicit total-budget key (total_rehab_budget/construction_budget/total_budget/budget_total); the budget tests use spent+remaining only or uploaded-doc types, never spent==explicit.

defects.boundary_changed: total += weight * clamp(val)  ->  total += weight * val
defects.edge_input: a component score outside 0-100, e.g. weighted_score({"a": 130.0}, {"a": 1.0}) or {"a": -20.0}
defects.wrong_output: returns the raw out-of-range value (130.0 / -20.0) instead of the clamped 100.0 / 0.0
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The one visible test that calls weighted_score feeds an in-range component (80.0) plus a None; clamp(80.0)==80.0 and engine components in the visible scenarios stay within band. NOTE: clamp remains used elsewhere in scoring.py, so this drop leaves no dangling import/definition tell.

resistance_assessment: Targeting Opus 1-4/10 (was 5/10 at 4 defects; Sonnet 0/5). A solve requires finding ALL six defects, so raising the count lowers the all-found rate (~0.5 -> aim 0.25-0.4). No local failing test points at any defect: the full existing qualification suite is green at base+6-defects, removing the run-pytest-see-red gradient. Five of six are single-token boundary slips (>=/>, </<=, > /  >=, len//2 vs (len-1)//2) that read as plausible code; the sixth is a dropped defensive clamp that leaves NO dangling import (clamp is still used elsewhere in scoring.py), closing the structural tell the 4-defect version had. Two defects sit inside resolve_document_fico (primary band + bureau median) but require independent realizations (an 850 edge vs an even-count parity); the construction-budget guard is buried in CTC-rejection logic and needs the spent==explicit realization. Residual fair discoverability: the tenant_concentration `>= 1` reads inconsistent against the sibling occupancy resolvers that use `> 1` — but nothing in any test signposts it.
